item.title: Staff Post- Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded:
edit: edit made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show
- hide

To link to more skips set in Japan, I propose the following edit:

SCP-2954-1A refers to six large pieces of weaponry resembling satellite dishes located on the boundaries of a deserted rural town in [REDACTED]

SCP-2954-1A refers to six large pieces of weaponry resembling satellite dishes located on the boundaries of a deserted rural town in [REDACTED]1

Footnotes
1. A known center of IJAMEA activity prior to the end of the Second World War.

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
Short and straight to the point, and I love the idea of a living Kaiju move happening every so often in real life.

https://youtu.be/_z3yiykPB0o

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Were the filmmakers killed by the creatures? If not, why leave the footage? If so, how was there already a poster?

Perhaps most importantly, how/why exactly is this happening? I don't need a clinical report on a new rando realitybender or somesuch, but it would be nice to have a little more mystery to sink my teeth into, such as 176 displays. I like the overall concept though, and having seen my share of Kaiju films, found it pretty entertaining. I'm leaning positive, but I'm going to leave it neutral for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Japanese filmmaker makes film using anomalous method, but forgot to clean up?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's amusing enough that I like it, but I can't really find anything worth an upvote. It's a bit too straightforward.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The monster is good, but:
1) you should also show 1-A. At least one of them!
2) Too bad I don't read Japanese… Oh, the title is Fukaeru's Assault!. Okay!

I like it anyway!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't have a whole lot to say about this one other than it's not much more than a straight-up skipification of kaiju movies. It feels thin.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh… It's just good enough to escape the mediocrity pit which would induce me to downvote — but not good enough to upvote. This is certainly not bad, and I do like that it has charm, but I wish there was more to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I made a quick drawing of how I envisioned the movie poster to kind of look like, if anyone wanted to see; click here to take a look!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it then and I still like it. It's well-written, fun, and nostalgia-inducing. The idea that the film shooting (ha, ha) continues 40 years later because no-one thought to inform the providers that they were done (or the providers didn't get the message, or chose to ignore it…!) is highly amusing. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The notes sold it for me. I was thinking "unending Godzilla" was cute, but not overly interesting, but the reason why it's happening (though it reminds me of another article I forget the nature of) is great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would say it's more Godzilla-ish due to the role of the military as a counter to said monsters. If it were Ultraman-ish, I would expect a giant humanoid fighting the kaiju.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The revision just hasn't done enough for me to upvote it. I'm still at a novote in overall thinking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding this, although I'm neutral voting rather than downvoting. There are probably a lot more creative ways to convey the twist that you could come up with, and it's rather disappointing to see the whole trope of "creator of skip leaves vague note that leaves just enough for reader to get the full picture" yet again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
2954-1 is A and B together, when one or the other is not specifically addressed (i.e. SCP-X-2 is killed from SCP-X-1's attack)

As for the other point, I can understand where you're coming from. Personally I wasn't going for a grand Godzilla-esque film; it was more along the lines of an Ultraman/Ultra Q atmosphere, where the monsters are less unstoppable, havoc-causing beasts, and more simply strange/natural-yet-frightening animals.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

life-threatening

Why not "lethal"? I do know it doesn't change the word count.

During Tsuburaya Events, SCP-2954-2 destroys at least one SCP-2954-1A

So what 2954-2 always destroy 2954-1A every time it manifests?

Pros:

  • I like the addendum portion that the makers are making kaiju films via replication.

Cons:

  • We know what are 2954-1A and 2954-1B, but what is 2954-1 defined as? It's hard to attach myself to the article if there's a component not addressed here.
  • While I like the subject-matter, I feel it does not have the essence of kaiju as a genre. Kaiju film settings are usually cities, and kaiju represent a destructive force of Nature (or atomic power in Godzilla's case).

Verdict: Downvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Competently executed, and certainly not bad, but I'm afraid I just didn't get much out of it… the "notes explaining the thing in a roundabout way" narrative device pushed that high-wire act of a neutral vote into a downvote, if only because I want to encourage people to try other things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't see a 2B… As for 1, 1A, and 1B, when it says just 1, it means both of them together; 2 is purely the creature that attacks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Your subdesignations seem to be a bit borked. You jump between -1A, -1, -2, and -2B.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Here's my short story contest entry! Shout outs to: TwistedGears, weizhong, Lex1nat0r, Dr_Leonard, DolphinSlugChugger, Soulless, EldritchCyanide, and DreamWalkerFae in chat, and for Eta Carinae and Dr Hysteria in the forums. /insert choice of kaiju roar here.
